title: Ayao Komatsu: Aiming High With Haas | F1 Beyond The Grid Podcast
description:
Haas Team Principal Ayao Komatsu is Tom Clarkson’s guest on this week’s F1 Beyond The Grid.

Ayao’s been with Haas since their debut season 10 years ago, but he’s only been in charge since 2024. So what has he learned about leadership in that time? How did he guide the team’s recovery from such a difficult start last season? And what are Haas’s targets in the new era of Formula 1?

As for the drivers, Ayao tells Tom what he wants to see from Esteban Ocon this year and how long they can hold on t

title: How Mercedes Outsmarted the FIA 🤯 F1 Loophole Explained #shorts #f1
description:
#f1 #f12026 #mercedes #formula1 #ferrari #fyp #shorts #foryou #lewishamilton #maxverstappen 

Mercedes may have just discovered one of the most controversial advantages in the 2026 Formula 1 season, and the entire grid is reacting.

In this video, we break down how Mercedes engineered a bi-phase front wing system that appears to comply with FIA regulations while still achieving aerodynamic behavior beyond the intended limits.

The FIA allows a maximum transition time of 400 milliseconds for acti

title: Game On At Mercedes? | Japanese GP Preview | F1 Nation Podcast
description:
As Formula 1 heads to Suzuka, Japan, a teammate battle is brewing. George Russell and Kimi Antonelli have driven the pace-setting Mercedes to one win each. How will they race each other this season? How will Mercedes handle their drivers? Max Verstappen has been linked to Mercedes in the past, so are George and Kimi also racing to keep their place in the team?

title: How Jacques Villeneuve Shook Up F1 | F1 Beyond The Grid Podcast
description:
The son of a motorsport legend, a racer since childhood, Formula 1 was Jacques Villeneuve's destiny. In 1996, the young Canadian arrived at his first Grand Prix and immediately proved he belonged there by taking pole position.

In his rookie season Villeneuve won races, went wheel-to-wheel with Michael Schumacher and took Williams teammate Damon Hill to a final race title-decider. 30 years on, he sits down with Tom Clarkson to remember how he shook up the sport.

title: 2026 F1 Chinese GP race analysis by Peter Windsor
description: New regs or not - McLaren-Mercedes in the race (or not): nothing could detract from the enormity of this day in Shanghai - from the first GP win for Mercedes' Kimi Antonelli. From the pole, he seized the race lead from Ferrari's Sir Lewis Hamilton on lap two - and was thereafter never headed.  Lewis re-applied pressure after the pit stops; and team-mate, George Russell, was up their in P2, pushing hard, as the race unfolded.  Kimi, though, was unperturbed. title: 2026 F1 CHINESE GP Sprint Qualifying analysis by Peter Windsor
description: No surprise to see the Mercs on the front row again but McLaren-Mercedes are now right up there with Ferrari.  Lando Norris qualified P3 behind George Russell and Kimi Antonelli, ahead of Sir Lewis Hamilton (Ferrari), Oscar Piastri in the other McLaren-Mercedes, and Ferrari's Charles Leclerc. Pierre Gasly was an excellent P7 for Alpine-Mercedes, beating a dismayed Max Verstappen (Red Bull-RBPT Ford). title: Dan Towriss: The ‘Passion & Drive’ Behind Cadillac F1 Team | F1 Beyond The Grid Podcast
description:
The newest team in Formula 1 are here to compete. Cadillac have earned the respect of their rivals. Now they’re pushing to get faster.

CEO Dan Towriss takes Tom Clarkson back to the start of the story - how he and his TWG Motorsports company first became involved in discussions about F1, conversations with General Motors and Cadillac, the work to build a team, choosing their race drivers and making their F1 debut in Australia.

Sergio Perez and Valtteri Bottas are the first Cadillac F1 drivers.

Tom Clarkson is joined in the Albert Park paddock by former F1 driver Jolyon Palmer and Channel 10’s Richard Craill to reflect on an entertaining season opener in Melbourne. 

Mercedes took the first victory of this new era of Formula 1, with George Russell leading Kimi Antonelli to a 1-2 finish. George might be the favourite to become World Champion, but could Kimi challenge him for the duration of the season
